characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Bare Midriffs Are Feminine: Inverted. His stomach is out at all times and he's a boy. Berserk Button: His last name is spelled "Generic" but is pronounced "Jen-Err-Rick" He'll correct you with annoyance. Big Guy, Little Guy: The little guy to his dad, brother, and uncle, respectively. Bratty Half-Pint: Mostly averted, but he does have his moments, such as the episode where he deliberately tried to sabotage Kelly's Sweet 16 party in favor for his own day he called Fort Day. Catchphrase:"Am I in trouble?"

characters.name: Bobby Generic
characters.description: Bare Midriffs Are Feminine: Inverted. His stomach is out at all times and he's a boy. Berserk Button: His last name is spelled "Generic" but is pronounced "Jen-Err-Rick" He'll correct you with annoyance. Big Guy, Little Guy: The little guy to his dad, brother, and uncle, respectively. Bratty Half-Pint: Mostly averted, but he does have his moments, such as the episode where he deliberately tried to sabotage Kelly's Sweet 16 party in favor for his own day he called Fort Day. Catchphrase:"Am I in trouble?"

characters.name: Howard Generic
characters.description: Fat and Skinny: He's the Skinny to Uncle Ted's Fat. Ink-Suit Actor: Is basically an animated version of Howie Mandel when he had hair. In-universe, he may actually BE Howie Mandel, as a few episodes of the first season had the live action Howie transform into the animated Howard on screen. Parents as People: As much as he loves his kids, he can be bossy, impatient, and a bit strict towards towards them, along with Martha. For example, After his grandma's antique table that he has worked on for 3 weeks was ruined by Bobby from playing around with styrofoam bags with his friends and getting the foam all over it, he gives Bobby a triple punishment in "Back to the Furniture". Pink Girl, Blue Boy: Inverted; he wears a pink shirt, while Martha wears a blue shirt and blue pants. You Are Grounded!: In the episode "Back to the Furniture" he took a triple punishment when he's a little boy.

characters.name: Martha Generic
characters.description: Longest Pregnancy Ever: While presumably not in-universe, she was pregnant with the twins starting in the first episode of the second season which aired in late 1991, and doesn't give birth to them until nearing the end of the third season, over a year later. Minnesota Nice: She has a thick Upper-Midwestern accent and a chipper personality, don'tchya know? Ms. Fanservice: Sports a few nice looking swimsuits throughout the show, including a blue bikini. Pink Girl, Blue Boy: Inverted; she wears a blue shirt and blue pants, while Howard wears a pink shirt. Verbal Tic: Martha frequently has phrases like "gee golly", "gee darn", "Fer corn sakes", and "Fer cryin' in the mud" in her sentences and ends some of them with "don'tcha know?" or "there, now, then, yet", owing to her Midwestern roots.

characters.name: Kelly Generic
characters.description: Bratty Teenage Daughter: At times when she doesn't want to do things with or be around the family. Catchphrase:"Like seriously? Get real!" Drama Queen: She tends to acts melodramatic over minuscule problems, such as claiming she would "totally die" if Bobby spoiled her Sweet 16 party. Drives Like Crazy: The rest of her family comment on her wild driving, and when she actually is shown driving, it shows! Justified, as she's still a teenager trying to get in the hang of driving. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Unlike Derek, Kelly, in spite of being rather aloof towards Bobby, is generally much nicer to him at times.

characters.name: Derek Generic
characters.description: Big Brother Bully: To Bobby at times, usually calling him "Dork" and "Dweeb". He is often portrayed as the villain in Bobby's fantasies as a result. Brutal Honesty: He never holds back his feelings towards Bobby whenever the latter's naivete gets on his nerves. Deadpan Snarker: He has a sarcastic streak, especially around Bobby. Delinquent Hair: Has a rat-tail hairdo to match his personality. Fiery Redhead: He has red hair and is the show's majorJerkass.

characters.name: Uncle Ted
characters.description: Affectionate Gesture to the Head: A running gag has him give noogies to Bobby in each appearance he makes. Catchphrase:"It's time for noogies!""Oh, that's a (noun)!"(when something goes wrong) "It's time for noogies!" "Oh, that's a (noun)!"(when something goes wrong) Fat and Skinny: He's the Fat to Howard's Skinny.

characters.name: Aunt Ruth
characters.description: Acrofatic: She was a former wrestler in her past, and she still has some moves in her.A purse thief learned that the hard way. The Dreaded: “The Visit to Aunt Ruth’s” makes her seem like she’s this but in reality, she’s a very doting aunt who even gives Bobby cookies at the end of his visit. False Teeth Tomfoolery: It is revealed in "Bobby's Tooth or Dare" that she wears dentures. When Bobby learns about how the Tooth Fairy takes teeth and leaves money from Jackie, he plots to hit the jackpot by stealing Aunt Ruth's dentures and hiding them under his pillow.

characters.name: Jackie Bodine
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer:Played With. She and Bobby are definitely friends, but he hates when she kisses him and wants her to stop thinking he's her boyfriend. Break the Cutie: Reduced to tears in "Bobby's Girl" when a frustrated Bobby shoves her. Girlish Pigtails: They're long enough to drag them on the floor. Girls Have Cooties: Even after he was told that they don't exist, this is one of the reasons why Bobby hates when Jackie kisses him. Self-Proclaimed Love Interest: Claims that Bobby is her boyfriend, much to his disgust.

characters.name: Meeker and Snerd
characters.description: Fat and Skinny: Snerd is the fat guy and Meeker is the skinny guy. New Job as the Plot Demands: Among the jobs they've had in the series include mall cops, paramedics, stagehands, pee-wee sport coaches, and airport security personnel.
